reasoning: The recent baseline of Explosions/Remote violence in Chile is exactly 1 event in 360 days (an average of 0.083 per 30-day window). To resolve positively, there must be more events than the baseline in a given 30-day window, meaning at least 1 event must occur. Based on a Poisson distribution with lambda = 1/12 (0.083 per month), the probability of at least 1 event in a 30-day period is approximately 8%. Given the sporadic nature of improvised explosives (such as infrequent anarchist bombings in Santiago) and potential for slight clustering or underreporting, I adjust this baseline up marginally to 9%.
